name: Loop from Nabainhos towards Freixo da Serra
name_original: Giro ad anello da Nabainhos verso Freixo da Serra
region_intro: Mountain biking around Melo offers a range of routes for varying skill levels, characterized by significant elevation changes and diverse trail lengths. The region's mountain bike trails include challenging climbs and descents, suitable for experienced riders seeking demanding terrain. Moderate options are also available, providing a balance of effort and enjoyment. Best mountain bike trails around Melo The most popular mountain bike trail is Folgosinho and Videmonte Loop, a difficult 27.8 miles (44.8 km) trail that takes about 4 hours 25 minutes to complete. This route features substantial elevation gain, traversing varied terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Loop from Nabainhos towards Freixo da Serra, a difficult 18.9 miles (30.4 km) path. This trail includes significant ascents and descents, offering a challenging ride through the landscape. Local mountain bikers also love the Folgosinho – Folgosinho loop from Gouveia, a 16.3 miles (26.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 7 minutes. Mountain biking around Melo is defined by varied terrain, significant elevation changes, and a network of routes that offer options for different ability levels. The routes in Melo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 8 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Melo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of mountain bike trails are available around Melo?
A: The trails around Melo offer a mix of moderate and difficult options, characterized by significant elevation changes. You'll find challenging climbs and descents, suitable for experienced riders, as well as moderate routes that provide a balanced ride. There are no easy trails listed in this selection.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly mountain bike trails near Melo?
A: Based on the available routes, there are no trails specifically categorized as 'easy' for beginners. The routes around Melo are either moderate or difficult, suggesting they require a certain level of fitness and skill. For those new to mountain biking, the moderate trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20141120" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Folgosinho – Folgosinho loop from Gouveia&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32135863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gouveia loop from Aldeias&lt;/a&gt; might be more approachable than the difficult ones.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails around Melo for advanced riders?
A: Advanced riders seeking demanding terrain will find challenging options around Melo. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2330546132/folgosinho-and-videmonte-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Folgosinho and Videmonte Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 27.8-mile (44.8 km) trail with substantial elevation gain. Another difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2330645981/loop-from-nabainhos-towards-freixo-da-serra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop from Nabainhos towards Freixo da Serra&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 18.9 miles (30.4 km) with significant ascents and descents.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the Melo area?
A: Yes, all the mountain bike routes listed for the Melo area are circular. Examples include the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2330546132/folgosinho-and-videmonte-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Folgosinho and Videmonte Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20141120" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Folgosinho – Folgosinho loop from Gouveia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of elevation changes can I expect on mountain bike trails around Melo?
A: Mountain bike trails around Melo are known for significant elevation changes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2330546132/folgosinho-and-videmonte-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Folgosinho and Videmonte Loop&lt;/a&gt; features over 1300 meters of elevation gain, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2330645981/loop-from-nabainhos-towards-freixo-da-serra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop from Nabainhos towards Freixo da Serra&lt;/a&gt; includes over 900 meters of ascent.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails around Melo?
A: The mountain bike routes around Melo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from the reviews received. Users appreciate the varied terrain and the challenging nature of the rides.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions near the mountain bike trails around Melo?
A: While mountain biking, you can explore several nearby attractions. These include historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4693289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aldeia Histórica de Linhares da Beira&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3899462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of the Old Water Mill&lt;/a&gt;. For natural beauty, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7203850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;São Lourenço Viewpoint and Ancient Oaks&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7240086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covão da Ponte&lt;/a&gt; river area. There are also religious buildings such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6661712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Igreja de São Pedro&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7156248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapel of Saint James (Serra da Estrela)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking around Melo?
A: The guide data does not specify the best time of year for mountain biking around Melo. However, given the significant elevation changes, it's generally advisable to check local weather conditions, especially during extreme seasons like winter, to ensure safe and enjoyable riding.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails suitable for families with children around Melo?
A: The available routes around Melo are categorized as moderate or difficult, and none are specifically listed as 'easy' or 'family-friendly'. These trails are generally better suited for riders with some experience due to the elevation changes and varied terrain.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking in the Melo area?
A: Specific parking locations are not detailed in the guide data. We recommend checking the individual route pages on komoot for more precise information on starting points and potential parking areas for each tour, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2330546132/folgosinho-and-videmonte-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Folgosinho and Videmonte Loop&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32135863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gouveia loop from Aldeias&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any trails that pass through the Serra da Estrela Natural Park?
A: Yes, some attractions near the mountain bike trails around Melo are located within or adjacent to the Serra da Estrela Natural Park. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/976298" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forest Trail in Serra da Estrela Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is a highlight in the vicinity, suggesting that some routes may offer access to or views of the park's natural features.
tips:
Gouveia is a picturesque Portuguese town and municipality located in the district of Guarda, on the northern edge of the Serra da Estrela Natural Park. With an elevated position that provides it with a mountain climate and picturesque views, Gouveia is considered one of the "gateways" to the highest mountain range in mainland Portugal. 

The town stands out for its rich historical and architectural heritage, with a charming old town characterized by narrow cobbled streets, traditional houses and monuments such as the Mother Church of São Pedro and the Paços do Concelho (Town Hall). Gouveia is also renowned for the production of the famous Queijo Serra da Estrela, a DOP sheep's cheese, and for its tradition of the Cão da Serra da Estrela dog breed. Its proximity to the Natural Park makes it an excellent base for exploring the natural beauty of the Serra.

Passable climb, but with a steep incline, from the EN (1200m) to the intersection (1500m) to the Chapel of S. Tiago (left) or towards the Old Forestry Services House (right).
Water source (fountain) in the first 400m.
The rocky massif that accompanies us on the left side throughout the climb is spine-chilling! The 360º views in the Chapel area are speechless.......

The village is famous for its granite houses, cobbled streets and its imposing medieval castle, which offers spectacular views of the surrounding landscape. 

Legend has it that Folgosinho was founded by Lusone, grandson of Noah, which adds a touch of mysticism to its already fascinating history.

Characterized by a serene and rural atmosphere, Freixo da Serra offers a taste of traditional Portuguese mountain life. Its stone houses, narrow streets and surrounding environment of cultivated fields and forests make it an ideal place for those seeking peace and contact with nature. Its strategic location allows easy access to some of the trails and points of interest of Serra da Estrela, while maintaining the tranquility of a less touristy settlement.
